visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video opens with a split-screen montage featuring a recording of a television broadcast. The top section shows a white block of tofu being released from a plastic container onto a ceramic plate after two taps on its corners. The bottom section displays a close-up of a Japanese man's face, wearing a dark suit, expressing intense surprise with widened eyes and slightly parted lips in a studio setting. (2) The scene transitions to a brightly lit modern kitchen where a young Asian woman with her hair in space buns, wearing a white top under a black apron, stands in front of a stainless steel refrigerator. She speaks animatedly to the camera, gesturing with her hands and holding a packaged tofu container, while smiling with high energy. (3) A medium close-up shot follows, showing the woman placing the tofu container upside down on a light blue ceramic plate. She uses her fingers to tap two corners of the plastic packaging firmly. The white tofu block slides out smoothly and perfectly onto the plate, leaving the container clean. (4) The woman reacts with an open-mouthed expression of extreme shock and joy. Digital colorful confetti graphics burst across the screen. She picks up the plate to show the successfully released tofu, looks directly into the lens with a bright smile, and holds up a peace sign to signal her success.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Subtitle]: 充填豆腐はするりと出る!. [Logo]: 緯來日本台 HD. (2) [Slogan]: 實測 讓豆腐掉出的方法. [Variety]: 只要敲兩次. (3) [Subtitle]: 看完日本節目說 只要叩叩兩下就可以把豆腐敲出來 我們就來實測看看可不可以. (4) [Variety]: 大為震驚. [Subtitle]: 欸 有效耶. (5) [Slogan]: 實測成功. [Subtitle]: 大家可以試試看 兩下！真的兩下就下來了. (6) [Logo]: 千千好吃驚?!

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator in the kitchen, 1 male guest seen in the TV broadcast clip.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with space buns, wearing a white halter-neck top and a black apron over her clothing.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A domestic kitchen with a large silver refrigerator, wooden countertops, and various kitchen utensils visible; a separate broadcast studio background for the TV clip guest.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio track opens with a brief, high-pitched Japanese male exclamation of shock, lasting less than a second. This is immediately overlaid by a punchy, high-tempo electronic pop BGM characterized by a bright synthesizer melody and a consistent 4/4 drum beat. (2) A cheerful female voice enters, speaking in enthusiastic Mandarin. Her speech rate is rapid, and her pitch is notably high, conveying excitement and curiosity as she describes the experiment. The BGM remains a steady presence beneath her clear, well-enunciated vocal tracking. (3) Two soft, percussive sound effects occur as the woman taps the container, followed by a resonant 'ding' sound effect as the tofu successfully drops onto the plate. These intentional SFX are crisp and timed perfectly with the visual actions. (4) The woman's voice peaks in arousal with a joyful shout of success. A festive 'pop' and digital shimmer sound effect play simultaneously with the appearance of confetti. The music swells slightly for a second before reaching a fast, clean finish as the video ends.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To demonstrate and verify the effectiveness of a 'life hack' sourced from a Japanese television program regarding the easy removal of tofu from its packaging.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The video focuses on testing a general kitchen hack rather than promoting a specific commercial product.

high_inference.freeDescribe: A typical social media 'fact-check' or 'life-hack' test video with high engagement cues and vibrant editing.
